businessSummary.oneLiner: World's largest EV maker and 'Physical AI Platform' spanning autonomous driving, robotics, energy, and custom chips
businessSummary.description: Tesla extends beyond EV sales with FSD autonomous driving, Cybercab robotaxi, Optimus humanoid robot, and Megapack energy storage. FY2025 revenue hit $94.8B with GAAP EPS of $1.08. Q4 2025 gross margin reached 20.1%, the highest in two years. Key catalysts are accelerating from April 2026: Cybercab production, Optimus Gen 3, and the Terafab semiconductor launch.
howTheyMakeMoney: Energy storage and generation (Megapack, Powerwall, Solar)
Services and charging (Supercharger, insurance, OTA upgrades)

keyMetrics.name: P/E (TTM)
keyMetrics.interpretation: Extreme premium over S&P 500 avg (23x) and auto sector avg (16x). Iran war and oil surge intensify multiple compression pressure

keyMetrics.name: Forward P/E
keyMetrics.interpretation: Based on FY2026 consensus EPS of $2.53. FSD regulatory setbacks across three markets raise the growth discount rate

keyMetrics.name: Gross Margin
keyMetrics.interpretation: Q4 2025 hit a 2-year high of 20.1%, signaling margin recovery despite pricing pressure

keyMetrics.name: Revenue TTM
keyMetrics.interpretation: FY2025 full-year. Slight YoY decline but energy segment driving growth

keyMetrics.name: FCF TTM
keyMetrics.interpretation: Solid cash generation. $44B total cash reserves to fund capex

keyMetrics.name: Net Margin
keyMetrics.interpretation: Compressed by pricing competition and R&D investment expansion

growthDrivers.title: $2.9B Chinese Solar Equipment Deal: 100GW US Production Target
growthDrivers.description: Reuters exclusive (3/20): Tesla negotiating with Suzhou Maxwell and others for solar manufacturing equipment. Targeting 100GW US solar by 2028. Texas delivery by fall 2026.

growthDrivers.title: Samsung AI6 Chip $16.5B Foundry Deal Confirmed
growthDrivers.description: Samsung confirmed at AGM: Tesla AI6 (HW6) chips to be manufactured at Texas Taylor facility using 2nm SF2P process. $16.5B through 2033.

growthDrivers.title: Terafab Semiconductor Unit Launches March 21
growthDrivers.description: Tesla officially launches Terafab today. Custom chip production for AI training, FSD inference, and Optimus robots. Reducing external dependency.

growthDrivers.title: Cybercab April Production + Macrohard AI Platform
growthDrivers.description: Cybercab production line designed for hundreds of units per week. Macrohard (Tesla-xAI JV) as enterprise AI revenue diversification.

growthDrivers.title: China Sales +35% YoY in Jan-Feb, Trump Visit Rescheduled
growthDrivers.description: China-made Tesla EV sales hit 127,728 units in Jan-Feb, up 35%+. White House announced Trump China visit within 5-6 weeks.

competitiveAdvantage.summary: 50%+ US EV market share leader. 8.2B FSD miles, Supercharger NACS standard, and vertical integration as core moats. Samsung AI6 foundry deal accelerates custom chip ecosystem.

moats.description: 8.2 billion FSD miles. Overwhelmingly more training data than competitors.
In-house batteries (4680), custom chips (Terafab+Samsung AI6), software (FSD), charging (Supercharger).
NACS is the North American EV charging standard. GM, Ford, Rivian depend on Tesla Superchargers.

competitors.name: BYD
competitors.detail: Global EV sales leader (2.26M vs Tesla 1.64M in 2025). 11 new models, 640-mile sedan, 5-min charging tech

recentNews.headline: Iran War Escalates: Pentagon Prepares Ground Forces, Strait of Hormuz Blocked
recentNews.significance: CBS reports Pentagon preparing ground force deployment to Iran. $200B war budget requested. Hormuz blockade cuts 20% of global oil. S&P 500 breaks below 200-day MA for first time in 200+ days.

recentNews.headline: FSD Europe Approval Delayed to April 10 Despite Testing Completion
recentNews.significance: Tesla completed 1.6M km of European testing, 13K customer ride-alongs, 4,500+ track scenarios. Approval pushed from March 20 to April 10.

recentNews.headline: Supermicro Co-Founder Arrested for $2.5B Nvidia Chip Smuggling to China
recentNews.significance: Yih-Shyan Liaw charged with diverting Nvidia AI chips through Taiwan to China. Signals tighter export controls.

recentNews.headline: Reuters: Tesla Negotiating $2.9B Solar Equipment Purchase from China
recentNews.significance: 100GW US solar production target. Texas delivery by fall. Energy vertical integration accelerating.

recentNews.headline: NHTSA Escalates FSD Probe to Engineering Analysis: 3.2M Vehicles, Possible Recall
recentNews.significance: Vision-only system safety concerns in limited visibility. 9 crashes (1 fatal). China also rejected FSD citing hardware redundancy.

analystOpinions.notableComment: 41-analyst consensus $406.84 (Hold). Upside expanded to 10.6% on price decline. BNP Paribas $280 (Underperform), BofA $460 (Buy), Wedbush $600 (Outperform).

risks.title: Iran War Escalation: Ground Forces, Hormuz Blockade, Oil Surge
risks.description: Pentagon preparing ground troops, requesting $200B war budget. Hormuz blocked (20% of world oil). Saudi intercepted missile. S&P 500 broke below 200-day MA. HSBC says markets pricing in recession.

risks.title: FSD Blocked in Three Major Markets Simultaneously
risks.description: US: NHTSA engineering analysis on 3.2M vehicles, possible recall. Europe: Delayed to April 10. China: Rejected for lack of hardware redundancy. Robotaxi strategy fundamentals shaking.

risks.title: S&P 500 Below 200-Day MA: Recession Pricing
risks.description: HSBC: 'Markets are pricing a recessionary outcome.' Fourth consecutive weekly decline. Worst environment for a 220x P/E stock.

risks.title: BYD Competition Intensifying
risks.description: BYD 2.26M vs Tesla 1.64M. 11 new models, 640-mile sedan, 5-min charging narrowing the tech gap.

risks.title: Supermicro Chip Smuggling: Supply Chain Uncertainty
risks.description: Supermicro co-founder arrested for $2.5B chip smuggling. Tighter export controls could indirectly affect Tesla's Terafab supply chain.

risks.title: Valuation Burden at 220x P/E
risks.description: 10x premium over S&P 500 avg. War and recession fears maximize multiple compression risk.

buyReasons.title: Terafab Launches Today (3/21): Custom Semiconductor Era Begins
buyReasons.rationale: Official launch today. No other automaker can produce its own chips. In-house AI5 production reduces Nvidia dependency.

buyReasons.title: $2.9B Solar + Energy Independence in Wartime
buyReasons.rationale: Hormuz blockade makes energy independence strategically vital. 100GW US solar capacity is a powerful long-term asset.

buyReasons.title: China Sales +35%, Only Growing Market
buyReasons.rationale: China market rebound plus Trump visit rescheduling raises hopes for tariff relief.

buyReasons.title: RSI Oversold, Down 25% from Peak
buyReasons.rationale: Historical rebounds from this level. But macro improvement is the prerequisite.

overallOpinion: The Iran war expanded to ground operations and the Strait of Hormuz remains blocked, pushing the S&P 500 below its 200-day MA. HSBC says markets are now pricing in recession. Tesla fell to $367.96, down 25% from its peak.

FSD faces headwinds in all three major markets simultaneously. NHTSA escalated its probe to engineering analysis on 3.2M vehicles, Europe delayed approval to April 10, and China outright rejected it. The robotaxi strategy's foundation is under pressure from all sides.

Terafab launches today (3/21) as the only near-term catalyst. RSI at 28 is deeply oversold, but technical rebounds are hard to expect amid war and recession fears. Wait for FSD resolution and war developments. Aggressive buying is risky here.

investmentVerdict.summary: The Iran ground war and Hormuz blockade are crushing the entire market. FSD is blocked in the US, Europe, and China simultaneously, creating unprecedented uncertainty for the robotaxi strategy. Terafab launch and April Cybercab production are catalysts, but prolonged war means inevitable multiple compression for growth stocks. Wait for war developments and Q1 earnings.

sources.name: Bloomberg - Market Today 3/20
sources.description: Pentagon Iran ground force preparation, S&P 500 -1.5%

sources.name: The Guardian - Iran War Impact
sources.description: Strait of Hormuz blockade, 4th weekly decline

sources.name: Electrek - FSD Europe Delayed
sources.description: FSD Europe approval delayed from 3/20 to 4/10

threads.hook: Tesla $368: War, FSD headwinds, and Terafab launch collide
threads.text:
$TSLA $367.96

Iran war expanded to ground operations with Hormuz blocked. S&P 500 fell below its 200-day MA. HSBC says recession pricing has begun. Tesla down 25% from peak, RSI 28.

FSD faces headwinds in all three markets. NHTSA escalated to engineering analysis on 3.2M vehicles, Europe pushed to April 10, China rejected it outright.

Terafab launches today as the only catalyst. But aggressive buying is risky until war and FSD situations clarify. Hold. Target $406.84 (+10.6%).

empathy.hook: Tesla holders, 25% down with war and FSD blocking. We see you
empathy.text:
$TSLA $367.96

$490 feels like a different era. Iran ground war, Hormuz blocked, FSD rejected everywhere. When it all hits at once, it's overwhelming.

But Terafab launches today. Cybercab in April. $2.9B solar underway. The company hasn't stopped.

RSI 28 oversold. When the war ends, the rebound energy could be explosive. Hold for now. Target $406.84 (+10.6%).
